roles.name: ui
roles.when_to_use: Buttons, labels, nav, forms, tables, dashboards
roles.note: System font stack is always acceptable — loads instantly, feels native, zero FOUT

roles.name: display
roles.when_to_use: Hero headlines, landing pages, splash screens
roles.note: Swap for your brand font. If no brand font exists, set equal to --font-ui

roles.name: mono
roles.when_to_use: Code blocks, API keys, IDs, terminal output
roles.note: Never use for prose or UI text

role_rules: NEVER use --font-display in UI chrome (buttons, inputs, nav, tables)
NEVER use --font-mono for any prose or UI text
If no brand font is available, --font-display = --font-ui (one font is fine)
System font stack (-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI) is always acceptable for --font-ui

weight_scale.use: Emphasis, headings H3–H4, labels, active states

weight_rules: NEVER use font-bold on body text — it reads like shouting
NEVER use font-bold at sizes below 16px — ink trapping reduces legibility
Weight alone is not sufficient to distinguish interactive from non-interactive text
font-medium (500) is the workhorse for UI — prefer it over bold for component labels
Use at most 2 weights in any UI component, 3 weights maximum across an entire page

letter_spacing.rule: Compensates for optical looseness at large sizes
NEVER change — font designer set this
Compensates for optical tightness of capitals

letter_spacing_rules: NEVER add positive letter-spacing to mixed-case text — looks amateur, reduces readability
NEVER add negative letter-spacing below 24px — characters collide
ALL-CAPS text REQUIRES tracking-wide or tracking-widest to be readable
When in doubt: tracking-normal is always correct

rules: NEVER use text-tertiary for interactive or meaningful text — fails WCAG AA
NEVER use text-primary for placeholder text — use text-tertiary
Color is NOT the only differentiator for interactive elements — always pair with underline or border
Semantic colors (danger, success, warning) must be paired with an icon — never color alone

text_alignment.rule: Only for ≤2 lines of short standalone copy

alignment_rules: NEVER justify text on the web — CSS justification creates rivers of whitespace
NEVER center-align paragraphs — only short, isolated copy (1–2 lines max)
Center alignment must be intentional and rare — if everything is centered, nothing is
